generated_for_sub_pass: §1.2 Supabase Callback Exchange Diagnostics 0.1 — PKCE Code Exchange Failure Boundary
diagnostic_conclusion: The callback route reads a query code and calls exchangeCodeForSession(code), but the configured Supabase browser client keeps persistSession false. Supabase PKCE requires the code verifier created during provider-login initiation to be available from the same browser/device at callback exchange time. With persistSession false, the package uses in-memory auth storage instead of durable browser storage, so the PKCE verifier is not expected to survive the full OAuth redirect round trip.
minimal_fix_not_implemented_reason: The narrowly indicated fix is to authorize durable auth storage/session persistence or an explicit storage adapter so the PKCE code verifier can survive redirect and the exchanged session can be recovered by protected-shell. That crosses the current accepted boundary and requires operator authorization before implementation.

evidence_summary: The PKCE exchange sends both the callback auth code and locally stored code verifier.
The code verifier is created and stored locally during provider-login initiation and the exchange must occur on the same browser/device.
The package stores the PKCE code verifier in auth storage before provider redirect and reads the same storage key during exchangeCodeForSession.
When persistSession is false, the package constructor uses memory storage instead of browser localStorage or a supplied durable storage adapter.

protected_shell_link_visibility_diagnost.classification: The visible header navigation to protected-shell.html is static site navigation, not protected shell admission. The callback-specific Protected Shell Boundary entry remains hidden unless session_present is true.

claimable_after_this_pass: Callback exchange failure boundary diagnosed against Supabase PKCE storage requirements.
No runtime auth persistence, implicit fragment handling, manual session manipulation, or Authenticated Surfaces birth claim was introduced.

next_threshold.name: §1.2 Supabase Auth Storage Boundary Decision 0.1
next_threshold.purpose: Decide whether to authorize durable Supabase auth storage/session persistence or another approved PKCE verifier transport before rerunning live callback/session recognition and protected-shell admission.
